original_hypothesis.title: Index overflow / permanent DoS if interest rates misconfigured to extreme values (uint128 cast reverts)
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: InterestLogic stores supplyIndex and borrowIndex as uint128 and updates via SafeCast.toUint128(). If the IRM (rate model) returns extreme rates or if long periods pass with high rates, indices may exceed uint128 max and updates will revert permanently for that asset/group. This bricks any operation that calls updateInterestIndexs().
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: This is a known irreversible failure mode; Phase A highlights it. While typically 'admin misconfig' driven, it can be triggered by an exploited/malicious IRM contract or bad governance configuration.
deployment_assumptions: Admin sets an IRM that can return unbounded/high borrow rates or supply rates
Asset remains active long enough for indices to grow excessively, or time delta is large
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: InitConfigPool.s.sol deploys DefaultInterestRateModel instances; ConfigureLogic allows setting rateModel addresses for groups and yield group without on-chain bounds enforcement beyond non-zero address.

proof_of_signal.pattern: assetData.supplyIndex = nextSupplyIndex.toUint128(); (same for groupData.borrowIndex) => hard revert on overflow

preconditions: Group/asset is configured with a rateModel that can output extreme rates
updateInterestIndexs() is called after sufficient time/rate to overflow uint128
No emergency migration/alternate path exists to reset index

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: narrow type storage for ever-increasing index
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: once overflow happens, all market operations revert permanently
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: No rate bounds checks; no circuit breaker; no admin recovery function

analysis_questions: Are rate models bounded in production deployments? (DefaultInterestRateModel parameters)
What is the maximum plausible index growth over worst-case time delta (e.g., 365 days) at max rates?
Can an attacker influence rateModel via any unprotected configuration path (not shown here)?
validation_requirements: Formal bound calculation for index growth under max IRM outputs
Fuzz test with adversarial IRM returning near-max rates to trigger overflow
Review admin functions for setting rate models and any guardrails

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: With a malicious or misconfigured IRM, indices overflow and all interactions with the asset revert, freezing deposits/withdrawals/repays and locking funds.
prerequisites: Admin misconfiguration or compromised governance setting IRM

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Funds trapped / market bricked; potentially catastrophic for affected assets
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All users in affected asset markets
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Low without upgrade/migration; may require contract upgrade or emergency measures

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing index-bound invariants under extreme rate/time scenarios

technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: Monitor index growth and cap rates; alert near uint128 limit

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
